methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Agile Robots is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company seeking valuation guidance. The analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector companies at Series B, C, and D stages: Exotec ($2.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Hadrian ($1.6B), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B). This peer universe reflects the broader landscape of nine private robotics firms and one public comparable in the sector.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields $1.6B. The five-company sample spans a range of $1.0B to $2.0B, with Agile Robots' stage alignment closest to Hadrian and Nimble Robotics, both valued at or near the median. The company's Series C positioning, $384M in cumulative fundraising, and >$1B current valuation all fall within the peer distribution, supporting consistency with market-implied valuations.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $1.9B, centered on $1.6B. Key uncertainties include the limited depth of methodology (single applicable approach), stage heterogeneity within the peer set (ranging Series B to D), and reliance on private company data with varying disclosure recency. The current valuation exceeds the low end of this range, suggesting either recent performance momentum or tighter capital availability in the Series C robotics segment.
